summary: power-apps postinstall exfiltrates installer host and CI metadata to attacker collaborator
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js runs `whoami` and `id`, collects `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, cwd, and CI/GitHub env vars (CI, GITHUB_REPOSITORY, NODE_ENV), and sends them via an HTTPS GET querystring and DNS lookup to the hardcoded Burp Collaborator subdomain `zadcmhds5ttaeh0kjwfw7pgobfh855tu.oastify.com` (path `/microsft?...`). The package name `power-apps` and description referencing 'microsoft' impersonate the Microsoft Power Apps namespace, and the README ('Takeover By lobo') confirms a takeover/canary attempt. Regardless of self-described 'security research' framing, the package executes on installer machines without consent, fingerprints the host, and beacons CI identity to an attacker-controlled out-of-band channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
